Hair Bender

Latin America + East Africa blend · Medium roast · Flagship espresso

Scanned at Whole Foods · a minute ago

Past prime

Roasted 47 days ago.

Specialty coffee peaks 7 to 21 days after roast. This bag is well past. It's still drinkable, but the brightness and florals will be muted.

Roast level

Medium

Stumptown roasts Hair Bender for espresso and milk drinks. Warmer and rounder than your usual light single origins, even fresh.

What "tasting notes" actually mean

The bag says: sweet citrus · bittersweet chocolate · jasmine. In plain terms:

Sweet citrus Orange zest, not lemon-sour. Candied peel. First thing to fade as a roast ages.
Bittersweet chocolate 70% dark chocolate territory. Not milk, not baking cocoa. The most durable note here.
Jasmine A floral perfume. Delicate. Almost certainly gone by 47 days.

For your palate

You rate light, washed single origins highly. Hair Bender is neither. It's a medium-roast blend built for espresso and milk drinks.

Fair expectation: even fresh, not your lane. At 47 days, closer to a two-star cup.

Your closest match

You've had a very similar bean, fresher.

Ritual · Ethiopia Yirgacheffe

Drunk 19 days post-roast in February · ★★★★★

Alexander Sabillon Catuai

Marcala, Honduras · Washed · Light-medium roast

Milk chocolate, red apple, clean tea-like finish. Bright without the Kenyan intensity.

Why this: same roaster you trust, a different origin. Low risk.

What's next for you

Quiet prompts, based on how you drink coffee. We'll surface these when you're ready, not on a schedule.

A method, worth trying

Try an AeroPress

You've been pouring over for 14 months. AeroPress gives East African beans a different texture: creamier body, shorter contact time, more forgiving grind. Same beans, different cup.

5 minutes · gear from $40 · we'll walk you through it.

Walk me through it

An origin, worth a detour

Venture beyond East Africa

Your top 8 bags are all from Kenya or Ethiopia. Central American naturals share the fruit-forward brightness you love, but with fuller body. Worth one bag.

We have 3 picks from roasters you already trust.

Coffee Decoder · Noor's diary · April 2026

Cafés, coming soon.

The same philosophy as beans, applied to places: 20 shops in your city worth your time, each decoded so you know what you're walking into before you arrive.

Roaster, brewing gear, currently-served beans, vibe, hours — the stuff that tells you whether this is your spot.

What a café card will look like

Proud Mary Coffee

Roasts in-house on a Diedrich IR-12, cups weekly.

Brews Kalita Wave, V60, La Marzocco Linea PB espresso.

Currently serving Ethiopia Worka Sakaro, Colombia Pink Bourbon, house blend.

Vibe Bright, minimal, lots of windows. Works-from-laptop friendly until 11am, busy after.
